metadata.title: Why partial JSON can be a valid object before the closing brace arrives
metadata.topic: Incremental parsing of streaming tool inputs
metadata.purpose: The parser keeps a token stack and, at each boundary, "closes" open structures with zero-value defaults — producing syntactically complete JSON from every prefix of the streaming source.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: full JSON edge cases (numbers, unicode), accumulator/event-loop wiring; the PHP fixture duplicate
metadata.visual_object: A token stream up top; below, a JSON tree whose nodes appear, fill, and seal as tokens land.
metadata.hook: A normal JSON parser throws on incomplete input, yet a streaming tool argument gives you a usable object mid-stream, before it's finished.

beats.narration_text: Streaming a `search_database` call, `.on('inputJson')` fires each chunk with a `jsonSnapshot` that is already a valid JS object even though the source string has no closing brace yet.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Standard parsers demand a complete document, yet each snapshot is a valid value built from an incomplete string — why doesn't it throw, and what does it return at each step?
A normal JSON parser throws on incomplete input, yet a streaming tool argument gives you a usable object mid-stream, before it's finished.
The parser keeps a token stack and, at each boundary, "closes" open structures with zero-value defaults — producing syntactically complete JSON from every prefix of the streaming source.
beats.visual: A token stream up top; below, a JSON tree whose nodes appear, fill, and seal as tokens land.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: `{"q": "solar` arrives in 4 chunks; snapshots are `{}`, `{"q":""}`, `{"q":"sol"}`, `{"q":"solar"}` — each a valid object no standard parser would accept.
The parser keeps a token stack and, at each boundary, "closes" open structures with zero-value defaults — producing syntactically complete JSON from every prefix of the streaming source.
